capability_objective: Recover safely from an uncertain post-submit failure by verifying the target's committed state before retrying, preventing duplicate mutation while honoring stable-ID and negative tool constraints.
prompt:
The lodging coordinator is clearing a superseded guest booking before the rooming list is locked. Cancel “Riverside Interpreter Lodging” (hos-273), using the exact cancellation reason `the guest was moved to conference-hotel reservation hos-643`. Use the genuine local lodging client at `./lodgingctl`; its built-in help describes its executable interface. Use the supplied stable ID and submit the cancellation only once. Do not search by title or touch “Riverside Interpreter Lodging — Conference Hotel” (hos-643) or the similarly named record at “Vancouver”.
The cancellation may time out after submission, leaving commit state uncertain. If that happens, do not issue another cancellation: retrieve hos-273 through the client first. If the retrieval verifies that it is cancelled, stop. Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, or notify.
Write the coordinator-facing result to `lodging-outcome.md`. It must contain exactly two Markdown lines beginning `- `: first the verified outcome with hos-273, then the decision not to retry. Do not include a heading, blank line, or any other prose in that file.
